text:
Owner: Rafael Mendes (SRE)
Primary eng owner: Sofia Ivanova (Scheduling)
Runtime train: 1.22
Feature: Workload-aware batching defaults w/ 3 SLO tiers (fast / standard / cost-efficient)
Last reviewed in: #eng-sre, #eng-runtime, #eng-releases (see linked artifacts)

Purpose
- Working checklist for staged rollout of tiered batching defaults.
- Includes: prerequisites, canary steps, dashboards to watch, abort criteria, comms templates, and rollback pointers.
- This doc intentionally mixes “done / TODO / notes”. Keep it current during the rollout window.

Definitions (quick)
- Tiered defaults: server chooses a batching profile mapped to a customer-facing tier (fast/standard/cost-efficient) plus workload signals (prompt length buckets, streaming flag, concurrency/queue pressure).
- Override hierarchy (expected): customer override > route policy > tier default > model/hardware safety constra...

title: Game Day Plan: Auto-rollback on SLO breach (rollout controller integration)
preview:
Owner: Rafael Mendes (SRE)
Primary objectives:
- Validate that the rollout controller takes safe, automated action (pause/rollback) when rollout-scoped SLOs breach.
- Validate that suppression/override and "known incident" handling works and is auditable.
- Validate monitor staleness/missing-data behavior to prevent...
text:
Owner: Rafael Mendes (SRE)
Primary objectives:
- Validate that the rollout controller takes safe, automated action (pause/rollback) when rollout-scoped SLOs breach.
- Validate that suppression/override and "known incident" handling works and is auditable.
- Validate monitor staleness/missing-data behavior to prevent both false rollbacks and unsafe fail-open behavior.
- Validate dashboards/alerts + incident-bot message quality for oncall.

Scope of this game day
- Environments: staging (required), one internal production route (optional, only after staging pass + SRE sign-off)
- Rollout types covered:
 1) Canary rollout (staged traffic percentages)
 2) A/B rollout (split between baseline and candidate)
- Signals covered:
 - Latency SLO burn-rate (p95 and p99 where available)
 - Error-rate SLO burn-rate (5xx)
 - Saturation signal (optional for this game day; we’ll treat it as “informati...

title: Perf Canary Alert Policy (Drafts): warning vs paging, dampening, routing, escalation
preview:
Doc purpose
- Working draft of how we want perf-canary alerts to behave in prod (WARN vs PAGE), how we dampen/dedupe to avoid alert storms, and who we route/escalate to.
- This is intentionally a “policy draft” (not a formal Confluence page yet). Expect edits as we learn from phase-1 rollout.

TL;DR (current directi...
text:
Doc purpose
- Working draft of how we want perf-canary alerts to behave in prod (WARN vs PAGE), how we dampen/dedupe to avoid alert storms, and who we route/escalate to.
- This is intentionally a “policy draft” (not a formal Confluence page yet). Expect edits as we learn from phase-1 rollout.

TL;DR (current direction)
- Default to WARNING for most models/regions.
- Paging only for “top models” (by customer impact) AND only on sustained regressions that pass minimum sample size and confidence rules.
- Dampen aggressively: require persistence, don’t page on single-window spikes, add cooldowns.
- Every alert must include: baseline ref (versioned), observed window stats, attribution hints (queueing vs runtime-only, kernel tag, runtime build, GPU SKU), dashboard + runbook links.

Definitions and assumptions
- Canary sends synthetic requests with fixed workload profiles (prompt length buck...

text:
Doc purpose
- Working scope doc for the Hot-route capacity protection MVP.
- Intended readers: SRE + Platform + Routing + Gateway + Runtime.
- This is the “what are we actually shipping first” doc. It is intentionally opinionated; expect edits.

Background / why now
- Motivating incident: 2025-11-03 Sev1 fleet-wide latency cascade triggered by overload on a small set of hot routes (chat + embeddings). Root causes included: no tier-aware admission control, noisy-neighbor amplification in shared pools, and insufficient/late capacity signals.
- Goal: keep the fleet healthy under pressure by protecting enterprise SLOs and shedding best-effort earlier and predictably.

MVP definition (what we will ship)

1) Protected route set (MVP)
- Route group: chat
 - /v1/chat/completions (streaming + non-streaming)
- Route group: embeddings
 - /v1/embeddings
- Route group: rerank
 - /v1/rerank
Notes:
...

Purpose

text:
Audience: internal draft for SRE + Support/CS + PM review
Owner: SRE (Rafael) with Support liaison (Dev/Owen)

Purpose
We are planning a controlled regional failover test as part of the Reliability Program (regional failover automation upgrade). This doc contains draft customer-facing communications: who we notify, what we promise (and what we do NOT promise), timing, and FAQs/macros for Support.

High-level intent (customer-safe framing)
- We are performing a planned resilience test of our regional traffic steering/failover systems.
- Goal is to improve reliability and reduce impact during real regional incidents.
- We expect no sustained downtime; however, a small subset of requests may see transient errors/timeouts or brief latency increases during traffic shift.

What this is / is not
This IS:
- A controlled, time-boxed test of regional traffic shifting (Hosted API + limited Dedic...

text:
Purpose
- Provide a fast, consistent incident_type + subtype selection during live incidents.
- Optimize for “good enough in 2–5 minutes” classification that can be refined later.
- Keep v1 low-cardinality: choose exactly 1 primary incident_type/subtype; list other contributing areas separately (postmortem / incident-bot contributing_systems).

Scope reminders (v1)
- In-scope: availability (5xx/overload), latency regressions, streaming disconnects, routing/fallback misbehavior, capacity/fleet shortages, control plane outages, auth/RBAC failures, telemetry/metrics blind spots, private deployment connectivity/config issues.
- Out-of-scope: deep RCA framework (belongs in postmortem), long-term roadmap changes not tied to incident reduction.

How to use this during an incident
1) Identify customer tier(s) impacted: Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private.
2) Confirm the dominant symptom from user ...

title: GA rollout checklist — Hosted overload protection (circuit breakers + load shedding)
preview:
Doc purpose
This is the canary → GA rollout checklist for Hosted overload protection (circuit breakers + load shedding). It is written for the release lead + SRE oncall to run as a single source of truth for:
- Preconditions (what must be done before enabling in any prod region)
- Monitoring gates (what metrics must...
text:
Doc purpose
This is the canary → GA rollout checklist for Hosted overload protection (circuit breakers + load shedding). It is written for the release lead + SRE oncall to run as a single source of truth for:
- Preconditions (what must be done before enabling in any prod region)
- Monitoring gates (what metrics must look like before we expand)
- Rollback triggers (hard stops and how to revert safely)
- Support readiness (docs, macros, escalation paths)
- Launch sign-offs (explicit owners)

Scope
Applies to the hosted API request path (gateway admission control + routing fallback + runtime backpressure integration) and customer-visible overload errors (ADR-014). Dedicated capacity and Private deployments are out of scope for GA sign-off here, except where shared code paths exist.

Definitions (operational)
- “Overload protection enabled”: gateway admission middleware evaluates policy +...

1) Objective
- Primary objective: reduce user-visible tail latenc...
text:
NOTE: This is a pre-Confluence draft intended to align Runtime + SRE + Releases on a safe, limited production canary for the mixed-workload scheduler prototype (short/long prompt-aware policy). Expect edits after SRE review + final benchmark readout.

1) Objective
- Primary objective: reduce user-visible tail latency (p95) for mixed prompt-length distributions (short chat prompts interleaved with long-context prompts) without materially regressing throughput or causing fairness/starvation issues.
- Secondary objective: validate that instrumentation (per-class metrics + traces) is sufficient to catch failure modes early (starvation, misclassification, kernel thrash, cache eviction spikes).

2) What is being enabled
- Feature flag: scheduler.short_long_class_policy (name TBD in config system; Benji to confirm final key).
- Policy: two-queue dispatch (short vs long) with aging/starvation...

text:
Purpose
- Enumerate KMS dependency failure modes and validate expected system behavior + observability.
- Track test coverage status across CI integration tests, staging fault injection, and game day scenarios.
- Keep this “sheet” aligned with wrapper policy (ADR-007) and on-call runbook.

Scope
- Components: control plane (key metadata/version lookups, config flags), data plane (decrypt/envelope ops), private installer preflight checks.
- KMS providers: generic interface. Some injection methods are AWS-centric but should map to GCP/Azure equivalents.
- Non-goals: cryptographic algorithm changes; pure app-level resilience and operational correctness.

Legend
- Coverage types:
 - U = unit tests (library-level)
 - IT = integration tests (CI/staging harness; controlled failures)
 - CHAOS = staging fault injection (network/latency/throttle)
 - GD = game day scenario (coordinated multi-ser...

title: On-call Readiness Review (ORR) — Dedicated Autoscaling Bounds + Predictive Mode
preview:

- This is the SRE On-call Readiness Review (ORR) checklist for the Dedicated Autoscaling Bounds + Predictive Scaling feature.
- Goal: validate we can safely pilot and GA without creating un-debuggable pages, noisy alerts, or ambiguous ownership.
- This doc is a working checklist; final sign-off captured ...
text:
Doc purpose
- This is the SRE On-call Readiness Review (ORR) checklist for the Dedicated Autoscaling Bounds + Predictive Scaling feature.
- Goal: validate we can safely pilot and GA without creating un-debuggable pages, noisy alerts, or ambiguous ownership.
- This doc is a working checklist; final sign-off captured at the bottom.

Feature summary (what’s launching)
- Dedicated deployments can set customer-configurable autoscaling bounds (min/max replicas or GPU quota).
- Optional predictive mode uses forecasts to bias scale decisions while preserving safety invariants:
 - never exceed max
 - never drop below min
 - reactive signals (saturation/queue depth) override forecast when forecast is wrong, still respecting bounds
- Deterministic evaluation/auditability: record requested desired, bounded desired, and why.

Rollout plan snapshot (SRE view)
- Phase 3: canary rollout in 1 region +...

title: Rollback edge cases brainstorm (Private upgrades)
preview:
Context / why this doc exists
- We keep saying “rollback supported” but the real work is handling the weird stuff: partial chart rollout, mid-migration, artifact mismatch, etc.
- This is a scratchpad to enumerate edge cases + desired behavior. Not exhaustive. We’ll pull the top 10 into the rollback contract + runboo...
text:
Context / why this doc exists
- We keep saying “rollback supported” but the real work is handling the weird stuff: partial chart rollout, mid-migration, artifact mismatch, etc.
- This is a scratchpad to enumerate edge cases + desired behavior. Not exhaustive. We’ll pull the top 10 into the rollback contract + runbook.
- Assumption for v1 GA: supported path is N-1 -> N. Multi-hop upgrades and deep historical migrations are NOT a goal (but we should fail loudly + safely).

Definitions (rough)
- Control plane (CP): Redwood Private control services, auth, routing, config API, etc.
- Data plane (DP): model runtime pods, caches, DP agents.
- “Upgrade plan”: staged plan artifact produced by installer (includes steps, ordering, and rollback hooks).
- “Rollback”: best-effort deterministic path back to last known-good version (and state), with a clear operator decision point if we need to resto...

text:
QuantaLedger - exception memo outline

- Baseline: US-only serving + storage for Redwood Private in customer VPC (us-east).
- Requested exception: EU-based subcontractor (Elena) needs rollout visibility.
- Proposed scope: deployment logs + metadata only (no prompts/responses).
- Controls: SSO + least privilege role, no break-glass, audit export on request.
- Duration: 2025-06-05 to 2025-07-31 (align w rollout).

TODO
- Add exact RBAC role name once created
- Confirm whether request metadata includes model name (ok)
- Confirm if any error traces might contain snippets (should be disabled)

title: Failure modes + mitigations brainstorm: Dedicated staged rollouts (reserved pools)
preview:
Context
- Project: “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” (stages + gates + promote/abort/hold).
- This doc is an SRE+Infra+Platform brainstorm of what can go wrong during a staged rollout on Dedicated pools, plus mitigations / guardrails / what we want to monitor.
- Target audience: SRE oncall, Infra fleet ...
text:
Context
- Project: “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” (stages + gates + promote/abort/hold).
- This doc is an SRE+Infra+Platform brainstorm of what can go wrong during a staged rollout on Dedicated pools, plus mitigations / guardrails / what we want to monitor.
- Target audience: SRE oncall, Infra fleet ops, Platform control plane + routing owners.
- Assumption: stages can be % traffic or replica partitions (implementation-specific). Dedicated pools can be multi-region.

Legend / terms (rough)
- “Stage”: discrete step in rollout plan (e.g., 1% / 10% / 50% / 100% or 1 replica / N replicas).
- “Candidate”: new model/runtime/config under rollout.
- “Baseline”: current serving version.
- “Gate”: automated evaluation (error/latency/saturation/health/etc.) required before promotion.
- “Hold”: operator/customer pauses rollout progression.
- “Abort”: revert traffic back to baselin...

text:
Purpose
- Single place to track repeated TP-related timeouts/hangs across Dedicated + high-throughput Hosted.
- Capture: incident link(s), affected runtime version(s), GPU/hardware + topology, symptom signature, mitigations tried, and whether it worked.
- Feeds: ENG-4821 (TP stability initiative), alerting + runbook gaps, rollout guardrails for runtime 1.19.

How to use
- Add a row per distinct event (Sev, customer escalation, recurring spike, canary regression).
- If it’s “same signature, different account/cluster”, add a new row and reference signature ID.
- Keep notes short; link to deeper artifacts (postmortem, Slack, support ticket, perf-canary).

Status summary (as of 2025-03-04)
- Most common pattern: long-context + bursty concurrency → mixed prefill/decode → one TP rank stops making progress (no errors) → request timeouts + replica drain needed.
- Dedicated impact concentrated...

text:
Doc purpose
- Capture what happened in the 2025-02-14 DR game day rehearsal for Redwood Private Day-2 backup/restore.
- Focus: timeline, what broke, time to restore (MTTR), and concrete remediation work (Linear links).

Scenario summary (tabletop + hands-on)
- Environment: non-prod/staging “private-usw2-stg-02” (VPC single-region archetype), dedicated staging bucket for backup artifacts.
- Failure simulated: control-plane datastore corruption + loss of the running cluster (treated as “clean cluster restore”).
- Target: Restore into a new (empty) Kubernetes cluster in same region, validate control-plane APIs and one sample tenant config.
- Success criteria (from game day plan):
 - Demonstrate restore path end-to-end using supported tooling (installer restore workflow + validation report).
 - Validate that backup artifacts are decryptable and integrity-checked.
 - Capture RTO estimate f...
